Glendalough Monastery is a renowned 6th-century early Christian monastic settlement in County Wicklow, Ireland, founded by St. Kevin. Located in a glaciated valley, it became a major center of learning and pilgrimage before being destroyed in 1398, leaving behind iconic ruins, including a 30-meter Round Tower and several medieval churches.

Sean's Bar is officially recognized by the Guinness Book of World Records as Ireland's oldest pub, dating back to 900 AD. It is a fully functioning pub with walls made of ancient wattle and daub, offering a unique blend of medieval history, live music, and a traditional, sloping floor that dates back centuries. Joyce Daze Extreme Slug Racing sticker can be found inside the pub. we stayed next door at the Bastion Bed and Breakfast, a haunted house from the 16th century.

P. Frawleys bar located in Lahinch on the west coast of Ireland. Lahinch is home to the Lahinch Trad Music Festival featuring many of the finest Irish Traditional musicians. Also featuring some of the finest drinkers I've ever met.
Home of Jamie Braswell's two sons, Conor and Alec and ex wife Kerry. We had tremendously great meals here!
Mom and Chai's first house together after their wedding.
Jacob and Maddy got married in Vegas! Crazy good time!
Ralpn Nugent, grandson of Francis A. Nugent of Forkhill Ireland was the last postmaster of Brickeys. The town was washed away by the Mississippi river shortly after his death.
Sam's childhood memories of life on the Akin's farm. Grace's sister Ethel married Richard Nugent's best friend Joel Akins

A Buddhist temple in Pa O Don Chai, Mueang District, Chiang Rai province, Thailand. Designed and constructed by Thai artist Chalermchai Kositpipat, beginning in 1996. Originally a dilapidated local temple, Chalermchai rebuilt it as an offering to Buddha and King Rama IX, opening it in 1997 with a unique white color signifying Buddha's purity.
Sam graduated with a Masters in Public Administration, 40 years to the month after his BA.
Fantastic history of the construction and loss of the Titanic. Includes history of the Irish rebellion and life of the linen growers.

Poulnabrone dolmen (Irish: Poll na Brón, lit. 'Hole of the Quernstone' is a large dolmen, a type of single-chamber portal tomb) located in the Burren, County Clare, Ireland. Situated on one of the region's most desolate and highest points, it dates to the early Neolithic period, with estimates from 3800 and 3200 BC.
Largest stalactite in Europe, 85 feet below ground

Newgrange (Irish: Sí an Bhrú) is a prehistoric monument in County Meath, placed on a rise overlooking the River Boyne. It is an exceptionally grand passage tomb built during the Neolithic Period, around 3100 BC,[3] making it older than Stonehenge and the Egyptian pyramids. Newgrange is the main monument in the Brú na Bóinne complex, a World Heritage Site that also includes the passage tombs of Knowth and Dowth, as well as other henges, burial mounds and standing stones.

Kilmacduagh Monastery in County Galway, Ireland, a historic monastic site founded in the 7th century.  The site features a well-preserved round tower, standing at 32.5 meters (112 feet) tall, which is the tallest in Ireland and notably leans about 2 feet out of alignment.

The "Black Temple" is the Baan Dam Museum (Black House), a unique art museum designed by National Artist Thawan Duchanee, not a functional temple. Located north of the city, it features roughly 40 dark wooden structures filled with unconventional art, taxidermy, bones, and horns, often representing a "hell" contrast to the nearby white temple.
920376169, 4464216

Wat Rong Suea Ten, known as the Blue Temple, is a stunning, modern Buddhist temple in Chiang Rai, Thailand, renowned for its vivid sapphire blue color, intricate gold details, and massive white Buddha image. Completed in 2016 and designed by Phuttha Kabkaew, a student of the White Temple’s architect, it represents wisdom and purity.

The Chiang Rai Cclock tower designed by renowned Thai national artist Chalermchai Kositpipat—who also created the famous White Temple (Wat Rong Khun)—the tower was built to honor the Thai royal family. Its intricate design showcases traditional Thai Lanna architectural styles mixed with contemporary artistry.
